author: Contrast Security
attributes.description: Correlates a WAF alert with a Contrast ADR confirmed exploit on the same source IP and URL path within 5 minutes. The correlation turns a low-confidence WAF detection (which may be in detection-only mode, or which may have failed to block) into a high-confidence, actionable finding. ADR confirms the application was exploited at the code level despite the WAF; the WAF event provides the gateway perspective. This rule is shipped disabled because the WAF index pattern varies per customer and must be configured before enabling.
false_positives: WAF in detection-only mode against high-volume traffic may produce alert pairs against legitimate scans or penetration tests.
WAF false positives on legitimate user input that happens to coincide with separately attempted application attacks from the same IP.
attributes.name: Contrast ADR: Exploited Attack Confirmed by WAF Alert on Same Request
attributes.note:
## Triage and analysis

### Investigating WAF-Confirmed Application Exploit

This rule fires when Contrast ADR confirms exploitation and a WAF alert exists on the same `source.ip` and `url.path` within 5 minutes. Treat as a confirmed exploit attempt at the gateway level that also succeeded at the application level.

#### Possible investigation steps:

- Review the ADR attack event: `rule.name`, `source.ip`, `url.path`, `http.request.body.content`.
- Check whether the WAF was in detection-only mode (alerted but did not block) or in blocking mode (alerted but the request bypassed). If blocking mode, investigate how the WAF was bypassed.
- Follow the `event.url` link on the ADR side to view full attack details in the Contrast Security console.
- Pivot to the matched WAF event to see the WAF rule that fired and any additional context (geographic, ASN, user-agent).
- Check for additional exploit attempts from the same `source.ip` across other URLs to scope the campaign.

### Response and remediation:

- Visit the runbook for the specific ADR attack type at https://contrastsecurity.dev/adr-runbooks/runbooks/{rule.name}/
- If the WAF was bypassed, escalate to the WAF team to tighten the rule. Consider moving the WAF rule from detection to blocking mode for this signature.
- Enable Contrast ADR Block Mode for the affected rule to prevent future exploitation.
- Block the source IP at the perimeter.
- Work with the development team to remediate the underlying vulnerability.
attributes.query:
sequence by source.ip, url.path with maxspan=5m
  [any where event.dataset == "contrast_security.attack_event" and event.outcome == "success"]
  [any where event.kind == "alert"]
attributes.setup:
This rule is shipped disabled and requires per-customer configuration before it can be enabled.

1. In the rule's `index` array, replace `logs-CUSTOMIZE_WAF_INDEX-*` with the index pattern for your WAF product. Common values:
   - AWS WAF: `logs-aws_waf.log-*`
   - Cloudflare WAF: `logs-cloudflare.firewall-*`
   - F5 ASM: `logs-f5.firepass-*`
   - ModSecurity: `logs-modsecurity-*`
2. Tighten the second EQL pattern if needed to match only your WAF product's alert events.
3. Confirm `source.ip` and `url.path` are populated consistently across ADR and your WAF product.
4. Enable the rule.
